sections.title: Limitations / Miscellaneous
sections.body:
he PF shall be a PIC holding an LFLB qualification for the RWY 18 VMC departure to join RWY 36 SID (Source: Training
Department Risk Assessment).

PF shall hold a current AHQ.

VPT RWY 36 is limited to DAY ONLY and 5 km visibility to ensure adequate visual reference (NTA limitation).
RNP Z RWY 18 is BALKED LANDING PERFORMANCE LIMITING. Balked landing performance shall be confirmed during
planning and before approach.
RNP Y RWY18 (AR) is PROHIBITED. (NTA does not have the required authorisation)

When ATC is closed one crew member must speak French (AFIS in English not available and airspace downgraded
Class G).  French speaking crew member should act as PM to maintain the situational awareness for the whole crew
(unless a safety related reason dictates otherwise).

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
10-10-2P2

-

sections.title: Overview
sections.body:
hambery airport is situated in a valley, oriented N-S, surrounded by mountainous terrain.
High terrain is very close to the airport (5200ft within 4nm on both sides of the valley and 6200ft 7nm east).

Overview of the Airport

Local area

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
10-10-2P5

-

lines: hambery airport is situated in a valley, oriented N-S, surrounded by mountainous terrain.
High terrain is very close to the airport (5200ft within 4nm on both sides of the valley and 6200ft 7nm east).

sections.title: Operational procedures — General
sections.body:
ue to terrain, strict compliance of published max IAS, min bank angles, and min climb gradient shall be adhered to.
NetJets has 3 tailored Jeppesen Charts for Chambery which can all be found under the CO Tab on the iPad EFB as
follows:
• 10-7: NETJETS RWY 18 VMC DEPARTURE TO JOIN RWY 36 SIDs.
• 10-7A: NETJETS SPECIAL AIRPORT (RWY 36).
• 10-7B: NETJETS Engine Failure Procedure RWY 18 RNAV SIDs.

THREATS:
ARRIVAL
DEPARTURE
RISK OF CONFUSION / LOSS OF SITUATIONAL
AWARENESS
Some approaches and missed approaches cannot be
flown by specific fleets. Ensure you are aware of the
aircraft capability and the contingency procedures to
mitigate this.

MOUNTAINOUS TERRAIN
CFIT risk. Procedures may require low speed / low
altitude manoeuvring during balked landings or go-
around. Follow briefing carefully. Pay particular
attention to performance calculations.

RISK OF COLLISION
Chambery is a busy airport which attracts a wide variety
of aircraft including GA, commercial, and helicopters.
Aircraft may depart on a northerly heading into the RWY
18 approach lane.
RISK OF CONFUSION / LOSS OF SITUATIONAL
AWARENESS
There are different contingency procedures depending
on the position of the aircraft after departure. Ensure
you are aware of different contingency procedures to
mitigate this.

MOUNTAINOUS TERRAIN
CFIT risk. Procedures of runway 18 may require low
speed/low altitude manoeuvring - 80/260. Follow
briefing carefully. Pay particular attention to wx and
VMC requirement where applicable.

RISK OF COLLISION
Chambery is a busy airport which attracts a wide variety
of aircraft including GA, commercial, and helicopters.
Aircraft may arrive on southerly heading into the RWY
18/ 36 departure path.
TURBULENCE AND WIND SHEAR
The following wind effects may be experienced:
• Wind from North, Northwest: possible windshear along LOC RWY 18 (+/- 5 NM from threshold), possible
downdrafts on short final RWY 36 between the motorway and the threshold.
• Winds from South, Southwest: significant turbulence around CH Locator associated with rotors.
• Wind from W, or when CBs are reported at 'La Dent du Chat': gusts 20-50 kts and crosswind may occur.

NOTE: The list of threats listed above is not exhaustive and any additional perceived threats and mitigation should be
briefed accordingly.

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
10-10-2P6

-

sections.title: Arrival procedures
sections.body:
.1 GENERAL
See THREATS
There are multiple approaches to LFLB. Refer to section 6.Weather / Airports Limitations for applicable Jeppesen
charts, minima, and reference sections.
Preferred approach is the ILS/LOC Z (due to traffic flow and Geneva airspace). Delays may be experienced if
attempting a different approach (consider extra fuel, performance permitting). Straight-in approaches are only
available to RWY 18.
The RNP Z is also available to RWY 18 however the balked landing performance is limiting.
RWY 36 requires a Visual Prescribed Tracks (VPT) procedure flown following an instrument approach to RWY 18.
For landing on RWY 36 see section 9.4.VPT RWY 36.

ILS/LOC Z RWY 18 initial approach and ILS/LOC Y&Z RWY 18 missed approaches require RNAV1 (RNAV1 navigation
specification requires the procedure must be retrievable from the FMS).

WARNING:
The RNP Y RWY 18 (AR) is prohibited for all NTA aircraft. This is because NTA does not hold RNP (AR) approval.

CAUTION:
ILS/LOC Y&Z, RNP Z and PAPI RWY 18 steeper than usual at 4.46 degrees. (Jepps 10-9 incorrectly shows 4.5 due to
rounding error). Do not use ‘steep approach’ procedures however do check and apply applicable AFM
supplement/limitations for 4.46°.
PAPI RWY 36 steeper than usual at 4.0 degrees and prohibited for use beyond 5 NM.

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
10-10-2P7

-
9.2. ILS/LOC (Z/Y) APPROACH RWY 18
See THREATS.
PROCEDURE:
As per Jeppesen.

MINIMA:
The ILS/LOC has minima that is derived by table on Jeppesen 11-2A / 11-2B / 11-2C. This requires IAS and Missed
Approach Climb Gradient (which takes into account the flight path to 5000 ft and turn degradation of 20 degrees).
To derive Jeppesen 11-2A / 11-2B / 11-2C minima:
a. Obtain missed approach climb gradient from table below;
Missed Approach Climb Gradient (%)
All Gradients at Max Landing Mass – except the XLS at 40°C which is limited to 17,000 lb

This complies with the required mean geometrical gradient during the entire flight path to 5000ft and accounts for turn degradation.
Includes penalty of bleeds for applicable types.
Assumes penalty of wing + cowl ice on for applicable types.

c. With the gradient and speed determined, use the appropriate Jeppesen chart 11-2A,  11-2B or  11-2C, to

CHAMBERY, FRANCE

10-10-2P8

-
PERFORMANCE:
For a straight-in approach to land RWY 18, ensure landing mass does not exceed the applicable dry runway
maximum take-off mass.
This ensures performance during a balked landing from straight-in RWY 18.
PDCS Settings: use ‘18DP5’ in ‘Departure’ along with actual conditions for the Go-Around and ‘DRY’ Rwy in
‘Options’.

CONTINGENCY:
Additional guidance is given in this briefing for the following contingency scenarios:
• Missed Approach ILS/LOC RWY 18;
• Balked Landing: Straight-in Approach RWY 18 from ILS / LOC Approach;
• Communications Failure.

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
10-10-2P9

-
9.2.1. CONTINGENCY PROCEDURES ILS/LOC RWY 18

MISSED APPROACH ILS/LOC RWY18:
As per Jeppesen.

BALKED LANDING RWY 18:

Straight-in Approach RWY 18 from ILS/LOC Approach
If a go-around is initiated after passing DA (MDA) then the standard missed approach may not guarantee terrain
clearance. The following procedure should be adopted:

All engines operating and missed approach climb gradient of 6% or greater:
• Climb straight ahead towards CH with maximum climb gradient.
• Cross CH at or above 3700 ft.
• Continue with the standard missed approach procedure.
Note: The required climb gradient to cross CH at 3700 ft is 14.8% from a balked landing at THR. This requires a rate
of climb of 2400 fpm at 160 kt or 2100 fpm at 140 kt.

Engine failure or missed approach climb gradient < 6% or unable to cross CH at or above 3700 ft:
• Climb straight ahead with maximum climb gradient.
• Follow the NETJETS Engine Fail RWY 18 RNAV SIDS (Jepp 10- 7B).
• If deviating from the standard Missed Approach, Inform ATC when practical.
Note: The 6% missed approach climb gradient referred to above is the same gradient as used to calculate the
approach minima.

LOST COMMUNICATIONS:
As per Jeppesen 10-1P2

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P10

-
9.3. RNP Z RWY 18
See THREATS.
The RNP Z RWY 18 is available at 4.46 degrees for CAT B and CAT C aircraft as LPV, LNAV/VNAV and LNAV.
CAUTION The Balked Landing Performance is limiting and critical. Carefully check balked landing performance
during planning and before approach.
CL650 is PROHIBITED from the LPV due approach angle (AFM Supp 11)

MINIMA:
The RNP Z has minima that is derived by table on Jeppesen 12-1A / 12-1B / 12-1C. This requires IAS and Missed
Approach Climb Gradient (which takes into account the flight path to 5000 ft and turn degradation of 20 degrees).
To derive Jeppesen 12-1A / 12-1B / 12-1C minima:
a. Obtain missed approach climb gradient from table below;

Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LFLB, ‘Runway’ to 18. And,
‘Departure’ to LFLB, ‘Runway’ to 18DP7.

Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
A. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LFLB and ‘Runway’ to 18. Input actual
landing weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’.
B. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LFLB and ‘Runway’ to 18DP7.
Input actual weather. Input ‘Flap’, and ‘Options’ as per table below.

Missed Approach Procedure: Immediately initiate a maximum gradient climb and follow the published missed
approach procedure.

Balked Landing Procedure: As per Missed Approach Procedure above, OR, if conditions permit (DAY, VMC, 5Km Vis),
perform a visual left-hand pattern (Max 150 Kts).

9.4. VPT RWY 36
See THREATS.
The VPT RWY 36 can be flown after ILS/LOC Z, ILS/LOC Y or RNP Z.

LIMITATION:
VPT RWY 36 is limited to DAY ONLY and 5 km visibility to ensure adequate visual reference (NTA limitation).

MINIMA:
The higher (most limiting) of the following shall be used as minima for the VPT RWY 36:

Engine failure or missed approach climb gradient < 6% or unable to cross CH at or above 3700 ft:
Climb straight ahead with maximum climb gradient.
Follow the NETJETS Engine Fail RWY 18 RNAV SIDS (Jepp 10- 7B).
If deviating from the standard Missed Approach, Inform ATC when practical.
Note: The 6% missed approach climb gradient referred to above is the same gradient as used to calculate the
The RNP Z RWY 18 is available at 4.46 degrees for CAT B and CAT C aircraft as LPV, LNAV/VNAV and LNAV.
CAUTION The Balked Landing Performance is limiting and critical. Carefully check balked landing performance
CL650 is PROHIBITED from the LPV due approach angle (AFM Supp 11)
The RNP Z has minima that is derived by table on Jeppesen 12-1A / 12-1B / 12-1C. This requires IAS and Missed
Approach Climb Gradient (which takes into account the flight path to 5000 ft and turn degradation of 20 degrees).
a. Obtain missed approach climb gradient from table below;
All Gradients at Max Landing Mass – except the XLS at 40°C which is limited to 17,000 lb
This complies with the required mean geometrical gradient during the entire flight path to 5000ft and accounts for turn degradation.
Includes penalty of bleeds for applicable types.
Assumes penalty of wing + cowl ice on for applicable types.
From the AFM/QRH/OM-B determine the maximum speed that will be used during the missed approach.  This
speed shall be suitable for a 20° banked turn.
With the gradient and speed determined, use the appropriate Jeppesen chart 12-1A,  12-1B or  12-1C, to
Interpolation of the Jeppesen minima tables is not permitted.
Performance PDCS setup: ‘Arrival’ to LFLB, ‘Runway’ to 18. And,
Max Landing Mass is to be limited to the lower of the following:
A. Landing Performance; Select ‘Arrival’ to LFLB and ‘Runway’ to 18. Input actual
landing weather and configuration, use ‘FLDTA’ in ‘Options’.
B. Balked Landing Performance; Select ‘Departure’ to LFLB and ‘Runway’ to 18DP7.
Input actual weather. Input ‘Flap’, and ‘Options’ as per table below.
Missed Approach Procedure: Immediately initiate a maximum gradient climb and follow the published missed
Balked Landing Procedure: As per Missed Approach Procedure above, OR, if conditions permit (DAY, VMC, 5Km Vis),
perform a visual left-hand pattern (Max 150 Kts).
Required PDCS Settings for checking Balked Landing Performance on RNP Z.

and reported weather conditions would necessitate anti-ice protection
during any of the published missed approach procedure. (Rwy 18 – 6,500ft
Always Set to DRY regardless of actual RWY State.
The VPT RWY 36 can be flown after ILS/LOC Z, ILS/LOC Y or RNP Z.
VPT RWY 36 is limited to DAY ONLY and 5 km visibility to ensure adequate visual reference (NTA limitation).

sections.title: Revalidation requirements
sections.body:
AUTION:
The DA/MDA of the straight in LDG RWY 18 IAPs vary with performance. If the DA/MDA derived from the ILS/LOC or
RNP Z is higher than the published VPT RWY 36 minima then the higher shall be used.

PERFORMANCE:
Max landing mass for RWY 36 as per PDCS.
Note: The VPT RWY 36 weather minima permits a visual pattern so RWY 36 Max landing mass does not need to be
further restricted for balked landing.

PROCEDURE VPT RWY 36:
As per Jeppesen 19-10 VPT RWY 36. This is a VISUAL MANOEUVRE in mountainous terrain. However, the following
waypoints may be used to aid accurate tracking during the VPT:
WAYPOINTS

LFLB1 (Point B)

N45 38.9 E005 55.1
LFLB2 (Final turn)

N45 36.7 E005 55.3

Waypoints are plotted on the CAT C prescribed track but may be used by CAT B aircraft (see pictures next page).

CONTINGENCY:
Additional guidance is given in this briefing for the following contingency scenarios:
• Missed Approach / Balked landing RWY 36 when visual references can be maintained.
• Loss of visual references during VPT RWY 36.
• Communications Failure.

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P13

-

WAYPOINTS TO ASSIST POSITIONING VPT RWY 36

Final RWY 36

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P14

-
9.4.1. CONTINGENCY PROCEDURES VPT RWY 36

MISSED APPROACH / BALKED LANDING RWY 36 WHEN VISUAL REFERENCES CAN BE MAINTAINED:
• As per Jeppesen 19-10, follow prescribed tracks for another approach; or
• If coordinated with ATC, follow localiser front-course outbound with max gradient climb to alternate.

LOSS OF VISUAL REFERENCES DURING VPT RWY 36:

Between point A and point B:
• Initiate a maximum gradient climb.
• Maintain Track 135° until D2.0 CY but do not overshoot D2.5 CY.
• Then turn right (25° bank) towards the runway; and
• Intercept QDR 355° CH and localiser front-course outbound.
• Do not accelerate until established on the localiser front-course outbound.

After passing point B:
• Start max gradient climb and stay within 3 NM DME CY.
• Then Turn right (25° bank)  towards runway; and
• Intercept QDR 355° CH and localiser front-course outbound.
• Do not accelerate until established on the localiser front-course outbound.

LOST COMMUNICATIONS:
As per Jepps 10-1P2

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P15

-
9.5. ALTERNATE ARRIVAL PROCEDURES:
See THREATS.

sections.title: Qualification requirements
sections.body:
INIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY.
PROCEDURE: If available, then request radar vectors towards final and, once visual, request a visual approach. If
radar vectors are not available, then proceed to the PIRUV HOLD, descend in the published hold to the MHA and, if
visual, then request a visual approach.

lines: INIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY.
PROCEDURE: If available, then request radar vectors towards final and, once visual, request a visual approach. If
radar vectors are not available, then proceed to the PIRUV HOLD, descend in the published hold to the MHA and, if
visual, then request a visual approach.

sections.title: Revalidation requirements
sections.body:
INIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY.
PROCEDURE: Follow the ILS / LOC Z, Y, & X (followed by VPT RWY 36 if landing RWY 36).
MISSED APPROACH:
-
‘At CH NDB execute a climbing 80/260 procedure turn left (bank 20, MAX 160 KT) to re-join CH NDB’,
with;
-
‘At D3.0 CY (past the station) execute a climbing 80/260 procedure turn left (bank 20º, MAX 160 KT) to
visually intercept and track the runway centreline northbound’.
-
Then follow the normal procedure as described in the applicable Jeppesen Chart.

lines: INIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY.
PROCEDURE: Follow the ILS / LOC Z, Y, & X (followed by VPT RWY 36 if landing RWY 36).
‘At CH NDB execute a climbing 80/260 procedure turn left (bank 20, MAX 160 KT) to re-join CH NDB’,
‘At D3.0 CY (past the station) execute a climbing 80/260 procedure turn left (bank 20º, MAX 160 KT) to
visually intercept and track the runway centreline northbound’.
Then follow the normal procedure as described in the applicable Jeppesen Chart.

sections.title: In-flight familiarisation syllabus
sections.body:
ecommended procedure for LFLB when airspace is downgraded to class G and AFIS in English is not available.
MINIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY / One French speaking Crew Member.
PROCEDURE:
-
VFR ONLY. When able cancel IFR with Lyon Approach 120.230 or 131.315 (East sector), do not cancel IFR
while still in contact with Geneva. Lyon will route you to CBY VOR and cancel IFR.
-
Refer to Jeppesen VFR charts 19-1 (Vicinity) & 19-2 (Airport).
-
Make position reports on 123.700 (Air/Air freq.).
-
Use IFR procedures' lateral and vertical profiles as guidance for situational awareness.
-
Fire & Rescue services monitor 123.700 & 118.300 frequencies.
-
Fire & Rescue services do a runway sweep 3 times a day.
-
Keep a good lookout for Gliders, Paragliders, and other VFR traffic.
-
Once on the ground close your flight plan by calling +33 5 57 926 084 (BORDEAUX BNIA*)
* BORDEAUX BNIA – Bureau National d’Information et d’Assistance au vol (National Aeronautical
Information Service)

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P16

-

he contingency is given on the Jeppesen chart 10-7B NETJETS Engine Fail Rwy 18 RNAV SIDS.
Due to noise and political restraints, the RWY 18 RNAV SIDs are normally only available Fri-Mon. On all other days
expect RWY 18 VMC departures to join RWY 36 SIDs. If weather is below minima for the VMC departure (Tue -
Thurs) check with ATC if RWY 18 RNAV SIDs are exceptionally available.

10.4. ALTERNATE TAKE-OFF PROCEDURES

Alternative departure procedures are given in this briefing for the following 3 scenarios:
A. Departure with CY LOC / DME Inoperative;
B. Departure with NDB CH Inop;
C. Departure when airspace is downgraded to Class G and AFIS in English is not available.

A. DEPARTURE WITH CY LOC / DME INOPERATIVE
See THREATS.
Detailed below are the differences from the normal procedures when the CY LOC/DME is inoperative. They should be
read in conjunction with the normal procedures to gain a full understanding of what is required.

lines: Alternative departure procedures are given in this briefing for the following 3 scenarios:
A. Departure with CY LOC / DME Inoperative;
B. Departure with NDB CH Inop;
C. Departure when airspace is downgraded to Class G and AFIS in English is not available.
A. DEPARTURE WITH CY LOC / DME INOPERATIVE
Detailed below are the differences from the normal procedures when the CY LOC/DME is inoperative. They should be
read in conjunction with the normal procedures to gain a full understanding of what is required.

sections.title: Qualification requirements
sections.body:
INIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY.
PROCEDURE: Request a visual departure to join the assigned SID at BANEK, use all available aids, particularly
the FMS to follow the lateral and vertical profile of a normal departure.
CONTINGENCY: Visually follow ground track of the escape procedure (Jepp chart 10-7A). The following
approximations may be useful:
D9.0 CY is approx. D6.4 CBY.
D5.0 CY is approx. D10.4 CBY.
See also the Visual overlay charts 10-7A.

sections.title: Initial qualification requirements
sections.body:
INIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY.
PROCEDURE: Request a visual departure to join the assigned SID at BANEK, use all available aids, particularly
the FMS to follow the lateral and vertical profile of a normal departure as depicted in Chart 10-7.
CONTINGENCY:
▪ ENGINE FAILURE BEFORE CH NDB: Visually make the turn onto track 325° at the position for D4.5
CY. This is shown on Visual overlay turn at D4.5 CY. It may also be achieved by timing for 1.5 NM
distance beyond the CH NDB.

Visually follow the remainder of the contingency as per 'DEPARTURE RWY 36 (CY LOC/DME INOP)' above.

▪ ENGINE FAILURE AFTER CH NDB:
o Follow the 80/260 procedure in Jepp 10-7.
o Establish on the R355° CH NDB outbound. Follow the remaining part of the contingency procedure
using visual guidance as per 'DEPARTURE RWY 36 (CY LOC/DME INOP)' above.

sections.body:
INIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY.
PROCEDURE: as per Jeppesen RNAV SID.
CONTINGENCY:
▪ ENGINE FAILURE BEFORE CH NDB: Visually make the turn onto track 325° at the position of D4.5 CY
which is 0.5 NM prior to LB620 (see also Visual overlay turn at D4.5 CY). Visually follow the remainder
of the contingency as per 'DEPARTURE RWY 36 (CY LOC/DME INOP)' above.
▪ ENGINE FAILURE AFTER CH NDB: Follow the SID.

B. DEPARTURE WITH NDB CH INOP
See THREATS.

sections.body: o impact, minima and procedures as per OM-C.

sections.title: Revalidation requirements
sections.body:
INIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / DAY ONLY.
PROCEDURE: Follow the procedure as per Jepp 10-7B. However, for an Engine failure before D4.5 CY, turn
at D4.5CY, maintain VMC and visually intercept / track the runway centreline northbound. Then follow the
remaining procedure as per Jepp 10-7B .

C. DEPARTURE WHEN AIRSPACE IS DOWNGRADED TO CLASS G AND AFIS IN ENGLISH IS NOT AVAILABLE (ATC NOT
AVAILABLE)
See THREATS.
MINIMA: Visibility 8 km / Ceiling 6000 ft / One French speaking Crew Member.
PROCEDURE:
• Pre-start, obtain enroute clearance by calling +33 5 57 926 084 (BORDEAUX BNIA*).
• If not possible, either contact Flight Dispatch for assistance or depart VFR and obtain airborne clearance from
GENEVA DEPARTURES 119.530.
• Brief OM-A 8.3.2. VFR Flight.
• Make position reports on 123.700 (Air/Air freq), including while taxing on the runway.
• Maintain VMC and clear of controlled airspace until IFR and clearance to enter controlled airspace is
confirmed.
• If IFR clearance is delayed, consider routing VFR towards the PIRUV hold above 6500ft (this is the main arrival
level at PIRUV for inbound traffic).
• Expect gliders, paragliders, and other VFR traffic.
• All other procedures and contingencies are as per previous paragraphs.
* BORDEAUX BNIA – Bureau National d’Information et d’Assistance au vol (National Aeronautical Information Service)

CHAMBERY, FRANCE
AIX-LES-BAINS
13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P21

13 MAY 2025 10-10-2P22

-
